_status: partially populated (2026-07-07 second + third research pass) - see data-requirements.md §17/§18 and ISSUES.md ISS-9/ISS-3

purchaseCost.note: 2022 government tender (Indian Embassy, Nepal): 200 hemodialysis machines + 60 RO systems, total INR 23.49 crore, implying INR 11.5 lakh/machine. Caveats: (1) bulk-procurement price (200 units) -- a private hospital buying 1-2 units may pay more; (2) the total bundles RO/water-treatment systems across the machines, so this per-machine figure likely includes some pro-rata site/RO cost, not a bare machine-only price.

usefulLifeYears.note: Companies Act Schedule II's only other category besides the named diagnostic-scan class (Cat-scan/ultrasound, 13yr) is 'other medical/surgical equipment' at 15 years. Dialysis machines aren't individually named by S8, but as therapeutic (not imaging-diagnostic) equipment they fall in this second category by elimination -- a direct inference from S8's own two-way split, not a separate research claim. See data-requirements.md §12.4/§14.

salvageValuePercentage.note: Companies Act Schedule II caps residual value at 5% of original cost unless separately justified. See data-requirements.md §18.2 for the citation-correction caveat (same as MRI).

installationAndAncillaryCostPercentage.note: GeM spec requires turnkey RO-plant installation but states no cost percentage. This range is the research pass's own inference from uncited 'anecdotal project reports' -- weaker evidence than the tender-mandated MRI/CT floors. See data-requirements.md §18.3.

warrantyYears.note: Machine only, excludes the RO water-treatment system. MEA/Nepal tender: 2yr warranty (parts+consumables). NHM Odisha pre-bid clarification: 3yr comprehensive warranty. The RO plant itself carries a separate 5-year warranty in a different GeM spec (see _roSystemWarrantyYears below) -- a different equipment item bundled in the same tender, not a contradiction. See data-requirements.md §18.1/§18.8.

_roSystemWarrantyYears.note: Warranty on the reverse-osmosis water-treatment system supplied with dialysis units, not the dialysis machine itself.

cmcYears.note: 5-year CMC for the RO plant following the warranty period. See data-requirements.md §18.4. Normalized to {low,typical,high} 2026-07-13 (PBA-1) -- a single reported figure, not a real range, so low=typical=high.

amcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
amcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Labour-only AMC. Generic tender clause (SCTIMST) applied identically across all 5 equipment types -- not Dialysis-specific. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

cmcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
cmcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Generic tender-ceiling range (GeM + SCTIMST caps), no cost percentages were found specific to dialysis/RO-plant CMC. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

_usagePerDayRange.note: MoHFW/NITI official planning guideline: three 4-hour shifts/machine/day = 3 sessions/machine/day. This is a DESIGN-CAPACITY norm, not necessarily real-world utilization -- the same source notes real-world utilization 'may be lower in smaller facilities'.

billedTariffPerUse.note: Hemodialysis (seronegative) CGHS reimbursement ceiling -- a government-scheme floor, NOT a private cash tariff, from a different-vintage CGHS document (nominally 2014, updated 2024-02-05) than the imaging tariffs elsewhere in this file. Seropositive sessions price higher (~1650/1898), sustained low-efficiency lower (~1250/1438). A private-provider source (S19, from the original pass) gives ~1500-4000/session patient-cost content but explicitly cautions against use as a default -- not used here.

launchDelayMonths.note: Still weakly sourced after a third pass -- not dialysis-specific. A general (non-dialysis) RO-plant installation FAQ states small/medium RO systems commission 'within days to a few weeks'; converted here to ~2-4 weeks (0.5-1 month) since RO water-treatment is the main bottleneck for a dialysis centre. Prefer a vendor-specific estimate. See data-requirements.md §18.6.
